steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage describes scientists who “later used” Weber's experiments “as the basis for their own investigations” that led to detection. The phrase “used as the basis” is the decisive clue—it indicates Weber's work was the foundation upon which later discoveries were built.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs a word describing work that serves as a foundation. Foundational directly expresses this—it's the adjective form meaning “serving as a foundation,” which matches the passage's claim that Weber's work was the basis for later discoveries.

The other three choices contradict this foundational role and the passage's positive assessment of his work.

That is the correct answer.
